cards.name: Powder Keg
cards.description: Deal [damage:5] damage. Ready after 2 turns. Speed up the timer when you use a Charge.

meta.nearestExisting: Cryo_Bomb.json (the shipped fuse-2 bomb: 6 dmg + 6 Frozen, Legendary); Bomb.json (the family template: fuse-3, 20 dmg)
meta.whyDifferent: Structurally the shipped Bomb pattern verbatim (cost-as-fuse: playCondition AnyValue IsLessThan [[costThisCard]]:1; StartPhase + SpendCharge reducecost:any:1; Draw setcost - all four lines copied from Bomb.json/Cryo_Bomb.json). Deliberately NOT a new decision by itself: it is the pack's token instrument, reward-excluded, sized under every shipped bomb (5 plain vs Cryo's 6+6-Frozen at the same fuse). The new decisions live on the issuer cards; the token joins the shipped 'bomb' keyword pool so every shipped bomb payoff (Rigged Explosives, Stockpile, Blast Junkie, Blast Zone, Tinker, Handcannon) reads it.
meta.intendedDeck: Conjured by Strike the Match / Sapper's Patience / Demolitionist Weapon. Matures in ~1 real turn under the Fuse-Cutter clock (StartPhase tick + one swing tick).
meta.powerNotes: Fuse-2 bombs shipped: Cryo_Bomb = 6 dmg + 6 Frozen (Legendary token). Ours: 5 plain damage, Common - strictly under Cryo at the same fuse; mass-issued instead of singleton. Detonation is a PLAYER decision at cost 0 (playCondition gates until ready, then it is a free Action) - the timing-vs-intents fantasy. All four codeLines + both keywords (OneUse+Persistent = Bomb.json's kw [31,36]) verbatim from shipped bombs; no uncertainty.

cards.name: Blasting Barrel
cards.description: Deal [damage:12] damage. Ready after 3 turns. Speed up the timer when you use a Charge.

meta.nearestExisting: Soulfire_Bomb.json (fuse-3, 10 dmg); Bomb.json (fuse-3, 20 dmg, cannon-kit economy)
meta.whyDifferent: Same verbatim Bomb pattern at fuse 3. Sized between Soulfire (10) and Bomb (20) because our issuers arm it for one cheap card with no Handcannon/restock economy behind it, and the Fuse-Cutter clock halves its real maturity. Token, reward-excluded - the decision (long bond vs fast keg) lives on Satchel Charge.
meta.intendedDeck: Conjured by Satchel Charge. The scheduling long bond: matures turn 2 under the full clock (StartPhase + swing + Satchel's own Charge tick).
meta.powerNotes: 12 at fuse 3 vs Soulfire's 10 and Bomb's 20 - inside the shipped band. Deferred-damage pricing per the fuse: ~12 dmg for 1 STR (Satchel) delayed ~2 turns vs the 1-energy ~4-dmg immediate baseline (CORPUS-STATS 1) - the overage is paid by delay, telegraph (foe may die first, fight may end) and hand-slot occupancy. All codeLines shipped-verbatim; no uncertainty.

meta.nearestExisting: Snare.json (enemy-deck plant: OneUse, 0-cost, self-damage on the drawer's own play); Glimmer.json (plant whose bless self-applies to the enemy)
meta.whyDifferent: Snare pings and cantrips (1+X piercing, draw 1, upgrade-scaling); Glimmer self-Charms. This is the first planted card that is a scheduled BOMB: a real damage payload (6) plus a self-applied hard stop (Stunned: 'cards cannot be played', Stunned.json) that ends the rest of the enemy's turn - a tempo detonation, not a ping. Phase-0 verdict: enemy-drawn cards do NOT fire on draw; planted cards fire when the enemy PLAYS them (Snare/Glimmer/Sabotage pattern, all PlayAction-trigger; enemy AI plays its hand in order, cost-gated - GAME-MECHANICS Part V), and a 0-cost plant is always played. The 'Surprise.' punchline survives exactly as the proposal's fallback intended.
meta.intendedDeck: Planted by Parting Gift. Works against every deck the enemy owns; best vs multi-action elites (Stunned cancels their remaining plays that turn).
meta.powerNotes: Payload sized as a delayed ~6 dmg + soft turn-break for Parting Gift's 1 STR: enemy deck is 20 cards (cloned deckList, Part V), so the fuse is drawn in roughly 1-3 turns. directdamage:6:normal:self is Snare's exact self-hit idiom; bless + referenceStatus on an enemy-played card self-applies (Glimmer verbatim: bless:2 Charmed); say is a shipped command (Hidden_Blade.json 'say:Requiescat in pace'; SpellEffects.cs:2383). LOW-RISK FLAG: Stunned is canNotBeIncreased (Stunned.json) - we apply exactly 1, no stacking intended; QA-check the enemy actually stops playing.

cards.name: Short Fuse
cards.description: Deal [damage:2] damage. Uses a Charge. Your Bombs' timers speed up.

meta.nearestExisting: Demolition.json (the shipped charge-card accelerant shape: STR 1, 3 charges, delves a bomb); Jab-class 0-cost commons
meta.whyDifferent: The tick rider costs NO codeLine: any card with charges > 0 fires the SpendCharge event when played from hand (SpellEffects.Decharge, SpellEffects.cs:12397-12410 - 'SpendCharge trigger for non-Equipment'), and every bomb - ours AND the shipped seven - already listens to SpendCharge with reducecost:any:1. No shipped 0-cost card exists whose entire identity is being a Charge emitter; the decision is spending a copy's limited uses (3/combat, then the copy is buried - depleted-card rule, EndActionStep) to compress a detonation into this turn instead of next.
meta.intendedDeck: Any bomb deck, including the vanilla Handcannon/Warrior cannon kit - it accelerates shipped Bombs identically. In-pack: the emergency throttle for steering a keg into the enemy's telegraphed turn.
meta.powerNotes: 0-cost common baseline ~2-3 dmg-equivalent (CORPUS-STATS 1). 2 dmg + 1 fuse tick, 3 uses per combat per copy (then buried for the combat - verified depletion path, SpellEffects.cs:615-645). Precedent for the charge budget: Demolition (C, 1 STR, 3 charges). Auto-Decharge on play + SpendCharge event are decompile-verified; no DSL uncertainty (the only codeLine is damage:2).

cards.name: Satchel Charge
cards.description: Conjure a Blasting Barrel. Uses a Charge. Your Bombs' timers speed up.

meta.nearestExisting: Lock_and_Load.json (U, 1 STR: conjure a Bomb + reload all Ammo); Restock.json (C, 1 STR: conjure 2 Bombs)
meta.whyDifferent: Lock and Load and Restock issue the cannon kit's 20-damage Bomb into a Handcannon economy (restock/ammo). Satchel Charge issues a smaller instrument (12) but is itself a Charge card: playing it fires SpendCharge (auto-Decharge), so the freshly conjured Barrel ticks immediately (arrives during the action, hears the end-of-action Charge) and every other held bomb accelerates. Arming that is simultaneously an acceleration event is a combination no shipped issuer has - the decision is sequencing arms to compound the clock.
meta.intendedDeck: Vanilla STR bomb/charge shell (Handcannon is a Warrior keystone weapon; Demolition/Toolbelt/Lock and Load/Rigged Explosives are acquirable STR cards) or the pack's fuse kit.
meta.powerNotes: 1 STR C: ~12 damage delayed ~2 turns + 1 immediate tick to all held bombs, 3 uses/copy/combat. Vs Lock and Load (U): less damage per bomb (12 vs 20), no ammo reload, faster maturity - not strictly better or worse; vs Restock (C, 2 Bombs = 40 total): far less payload, adds the tick - different decision (tempo vs mass). conjure:N + referenceCards is Restock-verbatim (conjure:2 + Bomb PPtr); charges-on-play tick verified at SpellEffects.cs:12397. No DSL uncertainty.

cards.name: Safe Distance
cards.description: Gain 4 Armor. If you're holding a Bomb, gain 2 more.

meta.archetype: defense inside the fiction - BRIDGE to Armor decks
meta.nearestExisting: Brace_for_Impact.json (C, 1-cost: 4 Armor, 8 if the enemy is holding an attack)
meta.whyDifferent: Brace for Impact reads the ENEMY's hand; Safe Distance reads YOUR armed bombs ([[bombsInHand]], the Handcannon-kit token) - the first defensive card gated on your own scheduled detonations. The decision it creates: holding a matured keg one more turn now has a defensive dividend, which is exactly the pack's timing tension (patience pays armor, detonation pays damage).
meta.intendedDeck: Any Armor/defense shell (Warrior armor piles) - 4 unconditional Armor at 1 Neutral is Brace-for-Impact-par and pull-safe with zero pack support. In-pack: the survival lane while bonds mature.
meta.powerNotes: Anchor: Brace_for_Impact (C, 1-cost, 4/8 on an externally-owned condition). Ours is 4/6 on a self-controlled condition - lower ceiling for an easier gate; not strictly better (6 < 8) or worse (same base, self-steerable rider). bless + referenceStatus Armor = the 441-card idiom (Block.json); AnyValue IsMoreThan [[bombsInHand]]:0 is Handcannon.json's exact condition (decoded conds [251:[[bombsInHand]]:0] family). No uncertainty.

cards.name: Parting Gift
cards.description: Deal [damage:2] damage. Add a Ticking Smile to your foe's deck.

meta.archetype: the screenshot card - a bomb in THEIR deck
meta.nearestExisting: Crossbow.json (basic: Ambush/Finale-gated 'Add a Snare to your foe's deck'); Rapier.json (Flanking-gated Glimmer plant)
meta.whyDifferent: Both shipped planters are position-gated riders on basic-attack weapons, and their payloads are pings (Snare) or self-charms (Glimmer). Parting Gift is the first ACTION whose whole purpose is the plant, ungated, and the first plant that is a bomb (6 damage + a Stunned turn-break when the enemy plays it). It extends the pack's thesis into the enemy's deck: their draw pile becomes one of your scheduling venues.
meta.intendedDeck: Any STR tempo deck - it is 2 damage plus ~6 delayed damage with no archetype requirement (pull-safe). In-pack: the punchline; pairs with Stunned tempo swings against multi-action elites.
meta.powerNotes: 1 STR U: 2 now + ~6 + turn-break delayed 1-3 turns (20-card enemy deck, Part V). Comparable to 1-cost U delayed-control precedent (Sleeping Poison's 2 delayed Jinxed). addcardtodeck:1:other + referenceCards is Rapier.json verbatim; the plant fires on enemy play (Snare pattern) - see Ticking Smile's block for the on-draw verdict. No DSL uncertainty on this card itself.

cards.name: Sapper's Patience
cards.description: Conjure 2 Powder Kegs. If you're holding 3 or more Bombs, draw a card.

meta.whyDifferent: Restock is flat mass supply for the cannon. Sapper's Patience adds the width payoff: the draw is gated on holding 3+ bombs, which only a deck that arms and HOLDS (patience) reaches - and every held keg is a hand slot not drawing cards (hand cap 10), so width has a real price the draw partially refunds. First bomb issuer with a bombs-held threshold; the decision is portfolio size vs hand liquidity.
meta.intendedDeck: Vanilla cannon kit (its Bombs count toward the gate) or the pack's fuse deck going wide under Chain Reaction / One Last Job.
meta.powerNotes: 1 STR U: 2 kegs = ~10 delayed damage + conditional cantrip. Vs Restock (C): 10 vs 40 potential payload but 2-turn instead of 3-turn maturity and the width cantrip - not strictly comparable; the rarity step buys the extra gate per CORPUS-STATS 1. conjure:2 + referenceCards Restock-verbatim; [[bombsInHand]] threshold = Handcannon/Tinker token, AnyValue IsMoreThan idiom. Degeneracy note for the sniffer: conjure here is on PlayAction (not the Conjure trigger) - no self-feeding loop; kegs mature over turns and hand cap 10 bounds width.

cards.name: Controlled Demolition
cards.description: Play a Bomb from your hand, even if it isn't ready.

meta.archetype: early redemption - cash out before maturity
meta.nearestExisting: Handcannon.json (Warrior keystone weapon, 2 Neutral: 'If you're holding a Bomb, play that instead' - playkeywordfromhand:bomb verbatim)
meta.whyDifferent: Handcannon is the launcher IN the basic-attack slot: every swing either hits for 4 or fires a bomb, an economy you buy at character creation. Controlled Demolition is the same verified mechanism as a pool ACTION: any deck that picks up bombs mid-run gains one-shot early redemption without owning the cannon. The decision is the economist's: pay 1 energy + a card to collapse a bond's remaining maturity to zero (against a telegraphed enemy heal/shield, or to dodge an incoming discard).
meta.intendedDeck: Vanilla cannon-kit decks (fires their 20-dmg Bomb early) and any deck that pulled an issuer; in-pack, the answer to 'the fight ends before my Barrel matures'.
meta.powerNotes: 1 STR U for tempo conversion, no raw stats - the value is entirely the held bomb's face, which the player already paid for (no double-count: this card adds 0 damage of its own). playkeywordfromhand:bomb is Handcannon-verbatim; implementation verified to PlayAction the bomb directly, bypassing the ready-gate playCondition (SpellEffects.PlayKeywordFromHand, SpellEffects.cs:10036-10048; selection UI when holding several). playCondition idiom (AnyValue IsMoreThan [[bombsInHand]]:0) is Tinker.json's exact play-gate. No uncertainty.

cards.name: The Long Con
cards.description: Deal {[[tempValue+1]]} damage. At the start of each turn this waits in your hand, it gains 3 damage.

meta.archetype: the card that lies - patience as a statline
meta.nearestExisting: Hidden_Flurry.json (in-hand tempValue growth: 'After you play a Sinister card, add one attack' - EndAction + addtempvalue while held); Bomb.json (in-hand StartPhase countdown)
meta.whyDifferent: Hidden Flurry grows per event of an archetype you drive; shipped bombs count DOWN in hand toward a fixed face. The Long Con is the inversion no shipped card has: a card that counts UP in hand, turning pure time into damage - a fuse where you choose the detonation size by choosing the wait, with no keyword support needed. New decision: it competes for the same patience the kegs consume, and cashing it early is always legal (1 damage - the pathetic jab it pretends to be).
meta.intendedDeck: Any deck at all - it is archetype-free and pull-safe (the growth needs nothing). Best where the pack already wants long turns held (fuse decks, control shells).
meta.powerNotes: Deal 1 on arrival; +3 per turn held: turn 3 of holding = 10 damage for 1 Neutral, vs 1-cost Rare par ~5 (CORPUS-STATS 1) - the overage is paid by 2-3 turns of a dead hand slot (hand cap 10) and draw-timing risk, same deferral pricing as the kegs. Linear, single-copy growth; no loop (sniffer-safe: no replay/copy commands). DSL: damage:[[tempValue+1]] and in-hand addtempvalue are Hidden_Flurry-verbatim (its codeLines: basicattack:[[tempValue+1]]; EndAction addtempvalue:1); StartPhase is in the engine's in-hand trigger whitelist (EventHandler.HandTrigger, EventHandler.cs:485 - same scan that runs shipped bombs' StartPhase tick). No uncertainty.

cards.name: Packed with Nails
cards.description: This combat, whenever one of your Bombs detonates, inflict 3 Bleeding.

meta.nearestExisting: Rigged_Explosives.json (the shipped 'your Bombs deal more' enchantment); Caltrops.json (per-event Bleeding enchantment)
meta.whyDifferent: Every shipped bomb payoff is MORE DAMAGE on the detonation (Rigged Explosives, Blast Junkie, Stockpile upgrades). This is the first payoff that converts detonations into a different archetype's currency: each blast loads 3 Bleeding, pulling the bomb deck toward the vanilla STR bleed/Deep-Wound shell (Lacerate, Cleavers, Cutting Words readers). New payoff for an existing setup (Difference mode 3) and an explicit bridge edge bombs->bleed that Part VI's web lacks.
meta.intendedDeck: STR bleed decks that pull one bomb issuer, or fuse decks splashing bleed payoffs; detonation count (not size) is the scaling axis, so it prefers many Powder Kegs over one Barrel - a real portfolio choice against Blasting Barrel.
meta.powerNotes: 2 STR Rare: ~3 Bleeding per detonation, realistically 1-2 detonations/turn mid-combat in a dedicated deck - proportional to Festering_Wounds' per-proc rate at U (its trigger is far more frequent). Trigger idiom: EndAction + PlayedCardHasKeyword Is 'bomb' is Hidden_Flurry.json's exact pair (EndAction t44 + cond [97:'sinister'] checking cardKeywords via ConditionChecker.PlayedCardHasKeyword -> CheckCardKeyword, ConditionChecker.cs:2326); enchant:self combat-blessing shape = Carnage/Salt-the-Wound pattern; inflict + referenceStatus = 441-card idiom. UNVERIFIED (rank 3): Hidden Flurry hears its trigger from HAND; ours listens from an active enchantment container (ProcessLastingEffects handles EndAction enchantment effects - Amber_Bomb ships an EndAction enchantment line, so the zone is exercised). Fallback if the keyword condition misfires in enchantment context: gate on AnyValue IsMoreThan [[bombsPlayedTurn]] equivalent is NOT available - fallback is Rigged-style adddamage rider (CalculateOutgoingDamage + CardContainsKeyword, fully verbatim) with the bleed moved onto Powder Keg itself.

cards.name: Chain Reaction
cards.description: This combat, whenever one of your Bombs detonates, spend a Charge. Your other Bombs speed up.

meta.archetype: cascade engine - detonations tick the portfolio
meta.nearestExisting: Blast_Zone.json (detonation-event enchantment: 'whenever you discard a Bomb, play it instead'); Stockpile.json (on-create bomb enchantment)
meta.whyDifferent: Blast Zone converts discards into detonations; Stockpile upgrades on creation. Neither touches the TIMER. Chain Reaction makes each detonation emit a Charge event, ticking every other held fuse - the cascade the archetype's fiction promises, expressed entirely in the shipped tick vocabulary (bombs already listen to SpendCharge). Bounded by construction: each detonation ticks others by exactly 1, matured kegs still need the player to play them, and conjured kegs cannot chain-arm themselves (no conjure in the loop).
meta.intendedDeck: Wide fuse decks (Sapper's Patience, Strike the Match, Demolitionist Weapon); also accelerates vanilla cannon-kit Bombs.
meta.powerNotes: 2 STR Rare engine: saves ~1 turn of maturity per detonation across the held portfolio - comparable to Rigged Explosives' +5-per-detonation at U priced as tempo instead of damage, plus the Unique/combat enchantment tax. UNVERIFIED (rank 1, the pack's riskiest line): 'decharge' exists in the closed vocabulary (effect-commands.txt line 196; SpellEffects.cs:1256 -> Decharge at :12397) but has ZERO shipped card users as a codeLine. In enchantment context cc = the enchantment's referenceCard (ProcessLastingEffects: 'cc = item.referenceCard ?? c', EventHandler.cs:305-360) = this card's own non-Conjured container with 0 base charges -> Decharge fires exactly one SpendCharge event and clamps charges to 0 (loop body, SpellEffects.cs:12397-12420); depletion-burial cannot trigger (that path only runs at play time for theCard.charges > 0). Fallback if the sim/QA falsifies: replace decharge:1 with directdamage:2:normal:self (Caltrops-verbatim shape) and retitle the rider 'the blast echoes: deal 2 damage'; card keeps its slot as a detonation-count payoff.

cards.name: One Last Job
cards.description: One Use: Deal [damage:4] damage. Spend all 4 Charges at once, burning every Fuse down.

meta.nearestExisting: Demolition.json (the shipped charge-spender family: each play = 1 tick); Big_Bomb.json (the shipped slow-payout ceiling)
meta.whyDifferent: Shipped acceleration is strictly 1 tick per Charge-card play. One Last Job is the first mass-maturity event: 4 SpendCharge events in one action (decharge:3 while holding 4 charges + the play's own auto-Decharge) ready every held bomb - fuse-3 Barrels included - this turn. The proposal's 'detonations are doubled' rider was cut (no verified doubling channel; multiplydamage:2 is the crit channel, Part VIII) and replaced with maturity collapse, which IS the fantasy: the whole portfolio comes due at once. The player still fires each keg by hand - the payoff is scheduling, not free damage.
meta.intendedDeck: Wide fuse decks; also readies vanilla cannon Bombs. The One Use + single copy per pull keeps it an event, not an engine.
meta.powerNotes: Legendary 2 STR: 4 immediate damage + up to ~5-17 unlocked deferred damage ALREADY PAID FOR by issuer cards (no double-count) + tempo. L anchors: 2-3-cost one-shot swings (Toxic_Bloom-class). Bounded: OneUse, charges 4, no replay commands (sniffer-clean). UNVERIFIED (rank 2): decharge as a codeLine has no shipped card user; in PLAY context cc = this card's own container (standard RunEffect cc binding), charges 4 -> decharge:3 fires 3 SpendCharge events (verified loop body, SpellEffects.cs:12397-12420), then EndActionStep's auto-Decharge fires the 4th and buries the depleted copy (redundant under OneUse). Fallback: playkeywordfromhand:bomb (Handcannon-verbatim) x1 + damage:8 - 'fire one bomb now', smaller fantasy, fully verified.

weapons.name: Fuse-Cutter
weapons.description: Deal [damage:1] damage. Uses a Charge. Your Bombs' timers speed up.

meta.archetype: the clock - every swing ticks every fuse
meta.nearestExisting: Handcannon.json (Warrior keystone weapon, 2 Neutral: 4 dmg / 'If you're holding a Bomb, play that instead' - the launcher); Warmace.json (2 Neutral for an ungated status rider); Blightpin Stiletto (sibling mod: the damage-1 stat-sacrifice precedent, Rogue menu)
meta.whyDifferent: 78-weapon sweep: 0 of 78 basic-keyword weapons carry charges (scripted scan, 2026-07) - the Charge-emitting basic is an unused combination (Difference mode 2). Handcannon FIRES held bombs (replacing the swing, at 2 Neutral); Fuse-Cutter never fires anything - it accelerates: each swing from hand auto-Decharges (charges 3) and the SpendCharge event ticks every held bomb's timer by 1, because shipped bombs already listen for exactly that ('Speed up the timer when you use a Charge' - their own printed rule). The weapon needs no new listener, no new command, and it accelerates VANILLA bombs identically. Same Warrior menu as Handcannon, opposite decision: launcher vs metronome.
meta.intendedDeck: The pack loadout (Strike the Match arms, swings mature, Demolitionist re-arms) and any vanilla bomb pickup; without bombs it is a weak-but-functional 1-damage basic - the honest floor of the sacrifice.
meta.powerNotes: Budget vs the 6 starting weapons (CORPUS-STATS 2: all damage 2, 1 Neutral, one rider): damage 1 = a full stat sacrifice (Blightpin Stiletto precedent) buying an every-swing rider that repeats all run - priced as a permanent engine per the weapon addendum. Additional hidden cost: each of the 6 deck copies has 3 charges/combat, then that copy is buried (depleted-card path, SpellEffects.cs:615-645) - 18 swings/combat ceiling, real only in marathon fights (flavor: the powder runs dry). Not strictly worse than any of the 78: no other weapon ticks timers; not strictly better: lowest damage line in the corpus and it does nothing alone. Auto-Decharge on play-from-hand + SpendCharge broadcast + in-hand SpendCharge scan all decompile-verified (SpellEffects.cs:12397-12420, :615-645; EventHandler.HandTrigger, EventHandler.cs:485). QA flag: commanded basics (basicattack:N cards) are not played from hand and do NOT tick - by design, only real swings are the clock; verify in gate 4.

weaponPowers.name: Demolitionist Weapon
weaponPowers.description: Use your weapon power to start your next combat with a Powder Keg in hand. Passive: Your Bombs deal 1 additional damage.

meta.nearestExisting: Deadly Weapon (cd 3: ActivateWeapon addnextcardfree + StartCombat passive - the exact structural template); Rigged Explosives (the '+X to your Bombs' line, as a card)
meta.whyDifferent: No shipped tier-0 talent touches the bomb pool at all (66-talent scan). The activation converts exploration downtime into an armed opening keg (the sapper prepared before the fight - thesis in one line); the passive is the run-long ordnance-quality dial that scales with detonation COUNT, rewarding the wide-portfolio plan over the single Barrel. Both halves point at the same archetype state, per the weapon-power addendum.
meta.intendedDeck: The pack loadout; the passive also buffs vanilla cannon-kit Bombs, so the power stays live if the run drifts toward Handcannon tools.
meta.powerNotes: cd-3 curve point (CORPUS-STATS 3): 'a start-of-combat card (choice-of-3 or free)' - addnextcardfree + referenceCard is Deadly_Weapon's exact cd-3 ActivateWeapon line (Talent scan; also Flywheel Weapon in the sibling Clockwork pack). Passive: adddamage:1 on CalculateOutgoingDamage + CardContainsKeyword 'bomb' composes two verbatim pieces - trigger+command from Deadly_Accuracy.json (t33 adddamage + ActionType condition, a shipped Talent) and the condition pair (58:'bomb') from Rigged_Explosives.json's enchantment; +1 is shipped small-rider scale (passives do not scale with cooldown, CORPUS-STATS 3). UNVERIFIED (rank 4, low): no shipped tier-0 talent uses CardContainsKeyword on t33 (both halves shipped separately); fallback: move the +1 into Powder Keg's face damage and give the talent a StartCombat bless:2 Armor rider (Deadly-Weapon-shaped, fully verbatim).

meta.nearestExisting: Powder_Pockets.json (Warrior keystone starting card: 'Conjure a Bomb. If you had no Bombs in hand, return this card to hand', 3 charges - found by the Phase-2 sweep; the ONLY bomb-touching starting card in the 63-corpus)
meta.whyDifferent: Powder Pockets is a keystone-unlock ENGINE: it returns to hand to re-supply the cannon with 20-damage fuse-3 Bombs and spends charges doing it. Strike the Match is a SEED: immediate tempo (2 damage - it is never a dead card) plus one fast keg (fuse 2, matures ~1 real turn under the Fuse-Cutter clock vs 3 turns for Powder Pockets' Bomb without one). Different slot in the run too: always-available mod loadout pick vs keystone unlock; and ours is the only starting card whose weapon completes it (the keg it arms is ticked by every swing). Reliability-first per the starting-card addendum: both halves are unconditional.
meta.intendedDeck: Loadout: Fuse-Cutter (the clock) + Demolitionist Weapon (opening keg + ordnance passive) + this. Mid-run pull: still a clean 2-damage-plus-delayed-5 tempo card for any STR deck, and its keg feeds vanilla bomb payoffs (Rigged Explosives, Stockpile).
meta.powerNotes: Starting-card curve (CORPUS-STATS 4): cost 1 (81% mode), Common, 2 effect lines, normal reward-pool card (NOT excludeFromRewards - 62/63 corpus rule). Value: 2 now + 5 in ~2 turns for 1 STR - at the 4-dmg 1-cost baseline once deferral risk is priced (fight ends, keg unfired = 2-dmg vanilla, the honest whiff mode). Both codeLines verbatim-shipped (damage:N; conjure:1 + referenceCards = Restock/Cogspark pattern). No uncertainty.
